DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF GREG HERMAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTNER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY AND FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST PLTF AND ITS ATTORNEY OF RECORD. MOTION GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. E-SERVICE OF PROCESS BETWEEN AND AMONG PARTIES REQUIRES SPECIFIC CONSENT OR A COURT ORDER, NEITHER OF WHICH HAS OCCURRED IN THIS CASE. SEE: CCP 1016.6. MOTION TO COMPEL IS DENIED BECAUSE EMAIL SERVICE OF THE SUBJECT DISCOVERY WAS NOT EFFECTIVE TO COMPEL A RESPONSE. THE MOTION IS GRANTED TO THE EXTENT SERVICE OF THE SUBJECT DISCOVERY IS DEEMED COMPLETED AS OF THE DATE OF THE HEARING. THE COURT RULED OR THE PARTIES STIPULATED WITH RESPECT TO DEFENDANT'S NON-SERVICE-OF-PROCESS RELATED OBJECTIONS. THE DEFENDANT SHALL HAVE TWENTY DAYS TO SERVE VERIFIED RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY AND SERVE PRIVILEGE LOG. BOTH PARTIES' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED. DEFENDANT SHALL PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 VIA EMAIL TO SBS@SBSLAWSF.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN STEIN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Off Calendar Jan-07-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Daniel Berko, esq. (415-771-6174) for Plaintiff.
Diana Cao, esq. (415-268-8000) of De La Pena Holiday LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF GREG HERMAN's Motion To Compel Furtner Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions Against Pltf And Its Attorney Of Record.

Motion granted in part and denied in part. E-service of process between and among parties requires specific consent or a court order, neither of which has occurred in this case. See: CCP 1016.6. Motion to compel is denied because email service of the subject discovery was not effective to compel a response. The motion is granted to the extent service of the subject discovery is deemed completed as of the date of the hearing. The Court ruled or the parties stipulated with respect to defendant's non-service-of-process related objections. The defendant shall have twenty days to serve verified responses to discovery and serve privilege log. Both parties' request for sanctions denied.

Defendant shall prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to sbs@sbslawsf.com.
